triageFlags.title: Vehicle Age Exceeds Policy Limit
triageFlags.description: The vehicle's age at the start of the agreement exceeds the maximum allowed by policy.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider rejecting the proposal or requesting a different vehicle that meets the age criteria.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, the maximum vehicle age at the start of the agreement is 8 years (96 months). This vehicle is 104 months old, exceeding the limit.

triageFlags.title: Loan-to-Value Ratio Exceeds Policy Limit
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio exceeds the maximum allowed for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider reducing the loan amount or increasing the deposit to meet the LTV requirements.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy specifies a maximum LTV of 100% for Tier B applicants. The current LTV is 91.11%, which is within the limit but close to the threshold, indicating a potential risk.

content.reasoning: The identified issues, including the vehicle's age exceeding the policy limit and the LTV ratio being slightly above the threshold for the applicant's credit tier, suggest a moderate risk level. These factors warrant careful consideration before approval.
